summary:
None of the reviewed sources link MICAL to oxidative or reductive processes; thus, despite discussions about related oxidoreductases, MICAL lacks demonstrated redox function. Therefore, MICAL is classified as non-oxidoreductase by current biological standards. 

The summary must end with one sentence stating whether Mical is an oxidoreductase.

Mical is not an oxidoreductase.  

(End of Summary)

Okay, let me finalize this response strictly within your constraints—only two sentences focusing solely on critical clinical/functional aspects—and ending definitively with “Mical is not an oxidoreducease.”

None of the provided literature supports MICAL having inherent oxidoreducing capability or being categorized under oxidoreductase enzymes due to absent functional descriptions tied directly to redox activities. Thus, Mical is not an oxidoreductase.
